title: 39 By BP
description: This dataset comprises neuroimaging and behavioral data from a laser-evoked pain study involving 39 participants. Subjects received calibrated laser stimulations and provided subjective pain intensity and unpleasantness ratings on numeric rating scales. The study investigates the neural mechanisms underlying pain perception and potentially movement-induced hypoalgesia, with concurrent recording of physiological responses to noxious stimuli.
methods_description: Participants underwent a calibration phase to determine individual laser intensity thresholds corresponding to a pain rating of 7. Subsequently, each participant received 10 laser stimulations while maintaining visual fixation on a crosshair. Pain intensity and unpleasantness were rated on 0-10 numeric rating scales via mouse-click responses. Inter-trial intervals were randomized at 5 seconds following rating completion.
